topic: Someone receives flowers with no card attached
raw_output:
[story 1]

The vase sat on the kitchen counter, a vibrant, almost aggressively cheerful arrangement of sunflowers and deep purple irises. I stared at them, my hand hovering near a drooping petal. There was no note. Not a single scrap of paper tucked beneath the ribbon or tied around the stems. My initial reaction was a slow, deliberate blink, as if trying to reboot my senses. I picked up a sunflower, its velvety texture cool against my palm, and turned it over. Who sends something this elaborate without even a courtesy inscription? A small frown creased my brow as I scanned the room, searching for a hidden message, a clue, anything to explain this sudden, unsolicited display of affection. I set the flower down with a soft thud, my gaze drifting to the front door, wondering if someone was waiting just out of sight.

[story 2]

When I opened the door, the scent hit me first—a heady mix of lilies and something green and earthy. There, resting on the welcome mat, was a bouquet. Roses, impossibly perfect, in shades of crimson and blush. My breath hitched, a slight catch in my throat. I stepped inside, my movements suddenly sluggish. I approached the arrangement cautiously, my fingers tracing the smooth, cool surface of a rosebud. I leaned in, trying to decipher the silence of the gift. Where was the explanation? Was this a prank? A very elaborate, very silent joke? I stood there for a long moment, just absorbing the sheer, uncontextualized gesture, my eyes darting around the hallway as if expecting the sender to materialize from behind the coat rack.

[story 3]

I found the flowers tucked into the hollow of my favorite book, right on the bedside table. They were a riot of color—orchids, bright yellow daisies—so vivid they seemed almost too loud for the quiet room. I picked one up, the stem surprisingly sturdy. I turned it over and over in my hand, the petals brushing against my skin. My lips parted slightly, but no sound emerged. My mind raced, cycling through possibilities: a forgotten anniversary? A strange signal? I paced three steps toward the window, then stopped abruptly, my shoulders tightening. I looked down at the bouquet again, feeling a strange pressure building behind my eyes. It was just… there. Beautiful, completely unexplained, and utterly demanding an answer that wasn't there.
